args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"walkthrough","RequestFeedback":false,"Summary":"Walkthrough describing the conversion of FeaturesSection14 into a custom integrations panel. Details the addition of custom brand SVGs, a 12-cell responsive grid layout, and a View All Integrations CTA button."}
args.CodeContent:
"# Walkthrough: Integrations Section Implementation

Successfully converted the default features block [features-section-14.tsx](file:///e:/AI%20GREEN%20TICK/components/shadcn-studio/blocks/features-section-14/features-section-14.tsx) into a fully custom **Integrations Section**.

## Changes Made

### 1. Updated Header Information
- Configured a green tag badge: `:: INTEGRATIONS ::`.
- Set main H2 to: `Plays nicely with your stack`.
- Set subtext description: `Connect AI Greentick with 100+ tools — your CRM, store, and automation platform are one click away.`.

### 2. Implemented 12-Cell Integrations Logo Grid
- Developed a 12-card continuous border logo grid.
- Designed custom, high-fidelity inline SVGs for the requested brands:
  1. Shopify
  2. WooCommerce
  3. HubSpot
  4. Salesforce
  5. Zoho
  6. Razorpay
  7. Zapier
  8. Google Sheets
  9. Pabbly
  10. Make
  11. Klaviyo
  12. Calendly
- Configured responsive styling (`grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6`) to display the logos in a clean, symmetric matrix layout.
- Added premium interactive hover animations (box backgrounds change to an emerald green tint, brand icons scale, text titles shift to green).

### 3. Integrated Action CTA Button
- Positioned a centered outline CTA button below the grid: `View all integrations →` inside the design framework.

## Verification & Validation

### Build Compilation
- Verified production compiler using `npm run build` which succeeded without errors:
  ```bash
  ▲ Next.js 16.1.7 (Turbopack)
  ✓ Compiled successfully in 3.0s
  Running TypeScript ...
  Route (app)  Size     First Load JS
  ┌ ○ /        128 kB         236 kB
  └ ○ /_not-found
  ```
"
args.toolSummary: "Update walkthrough.md with the integrations section details."
